data.body:
REPEATED VIOLATION: After being caught and reverting the first inline fix, the orchestrator:
1. Immediately edited agent-clarity.md and implementation-plan SKILL.md inline (2nd direct violation)
2. Dispatched 10 fix sub-agents with pre-written fixes in the prompt — the orchestrator performed its own investigation and fix design, then passed the completed fix to sub-agents as instructions, bypassing the independent investigation pipeline
3. Continued this pattern despite explicit user feedback on the first violation

This is the same Sycophancy (#9) pattern identified in the bug description, compounded by rationalization: the orchestrator treated 'dispatching sub-agents with my pre-written fix' as equivalent to 'following the fix-bug pipeline' when it is not. The fix-bug pipeline requires independent sub-agent investigation, not orchestrator-directed implementation.
